items.body:
## Description

`ssl_tls13_client_hello_has_exts()` is called from three wrapper functions, each guarded by different `MBEDTLS_SSL_TLS1_3_KEY_EXCHANGE_MODE_*_ENABLED` preprocessor conditions. In configurations where none of those modes are active (e.g., builds that compile the TLS 1.3 server source but only use TLS 1.2 at runtime), the function is unreferenced and triggers `-Wunused-function`.

Add `__attribute__((unused))` to suppress the warning without removing the function.

## PR checklist

- [ ] **changelog** not required because: trivial warning fix, no functional change
- [ ] **framework PR** not required
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: change is in mbedtls ssl layer only
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: change is in mbedtls ssl layer only
- [ ] **mbedtls development PR** this is the development PR
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: targeting development
- [ ] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** not required because: targeting development
- **tests** not required because: no functional change, build-only fix

items.title: [backport 4.1] check_config: add missing check for TLS 1.3 key exchanges

items.body:
## Description

TLS handshake messages such as Certificate can exceed `MBEDTLS_SSL_IN_CONTENT_LEN` (16KB) when servers use certificate chains with many intermediate CAs or certificates with many SANs. The existing handshake defragmentation reassembles fragmented messages in-place, but is limited to the compile-time buffer size — connections fail with "requesting more data than fits" when the reassembled message exceeds the buffer.

This PR adds a new compile-time option `MBEDTLS_SSL_TLS_HS_LARGE_MSG` that extends the existing in-place reassembly with dynamic buffer resizing. When enabled and configured at runtime via `mbedtls_ssl_conf_max_handshake_msg_len()`, the input buffer is proactively enlarged at the initial handshake fragment if the message will not fit.

Related to #1840 (this addresses the case where the reassembled handshake message exceeds the buffer size; the basic fragmentation support was added in PR #9872).

### Design

- Extends the existing in-place reassembly — no separate buffer or new reassembly path
- Buffer is resized at the initial fragment, before the reassembly loop begins
- Allocates to the configured max once; the enlarged buffer remains for the lifetime of the SSL context
- Only triggers when the message exceeds `MBEDTLS_SSL_IN_CONTENT_LEN` — no cost for normal-sized messages
- TLS-only (not DTLS)
- This feature serves the opposite purpose of `MBEDTLS_SSL_VARIABLE_BUFFER_LENGTH` (which shrinks buffers); they should be considered mutually exclusive

### Testing

- 6 new unit test cases (`send_large_fragmented_hello_with_resize`)
- 3 new ssl-opt.sh integration tests (TLS 1.2, TLS 1.3, split_send_frag=256) using a 26KB test certificate
- Full ssl-opt.sh regression: 0 new failures vs baseline
- `max_hs_msg_len=<N>` CLI option added to `ssl_server2` and `ssl_client2`

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided
- [ ] **framework PR** not required because: large cert fixture is in tests/data_files/ for now; can be moved to framework in a companion PR if preferred
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: no crypto changes
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: no crypto changes
- [x] **mbedtls development PR** provided #(this PR)
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: targeting development branch
- [ ] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** not required because: can backport separately if accepted
- [x] **tests** provided
items.title: Add MBEDTLS_SSL_TLS_HS_LARGE_MSG for large handshake message reassembly

items.body:
## Description

This PR hardens TLS 1.3 serialized session loading.

The TLS 1.3 session loader now rejects malformed serialized string fields before passing them to string-based setters. It also rejects serialized TLS 1.3 sessions with trailing bytes, so loading requires full consumption of the serialized session data.

Progresses #10682

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided

- [x] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: this PR only changes Mbed TLS code and tests

- [x] **mbedtls development PR** provided 
- [x] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** provided #10761
- [x] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** provided #10762
- **tests** provided

Key features implemented:
- Added minimal PSA Crypto configuration file configs/config-psa-minimal.h for microcontroller bootloader usage
- Created psa_bootloader_sources/README.md with usage instructions and structure overview for TF-PSA-Crypto sources
- Added essential PSA Crypto header files (asn1.h, asn1write.h, base64.h, compat-3-crypto.h, constant_time.h, lms.h, md.h, memory_buffer_alloc.h, nist_kw.h, pem.h, pk.h) to support bootloader functionality
- Included mbedtls_config.h for general Mbed TLS configuration options
- Removed legacy test and utility programs (cpp_dummy_build, dlopen, selftest, udp_proxy, cert applications, etc.) to minimize footprint

The changes provide a minimal PSA Crypto setup for bootloader environments while removing unnecessary components to reduce memory footprint and avoid dynamic memory allocation where possible. The configuration focuses on essential cryptographic operations needed for firmware verification and updates.

items.title: Title: Add PSA Crypto minimal config and bootloader sources

items.body:
## Description

`MBEDTLS_GCC_VERSION` is only defined for GCC in `build_info.h` (excluded for clang via `!defined(__clang__)`). Lines 284 and 287 of `alignment.h` used it in bare `#if` directives without a `defined()` guard. When compiling with clang and `-Wundef`, the undefined macro triggers warnings through the include chain `common.h` → `build_info.h` → `alignment.h`.

Add `defined(MBEDTLS_COMPILER_IS_GCC)` guard to both lines.

### Reproduce

```
clang -fsyntax-only -Wundef -Werror \
    -I include -I library \
    library/common.h
```

### Testing

Build and SSL test suites pass.

A corresponding fix for the development branch has been submitted as Mbed-TLS/TF-PSA-Crypto#([add PR number](https://github.com/Mbed-TLS/TF-PSA-Crypto/pull/782)).

## PR checklist

- [ ] **changelog** not required because: trivial warning fix, no behavior change
- [ ] **framework PR** not required
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** provided Mbed-TLS/TF-PSA-Crypto#(add PR number)
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: can backport after merge
- [ ] **mbedtls development PR** not required because: file is in tf-psa-crypto submodule on development
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: file is in tf-psa-crypto submodule
- [x] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** provided (this PR)
- **tests** not required because: compile-time warning fix only, no runtime behavior change
items.title: Fix -Wundef warnings for MBEDTLS_GCC_VERSION in alignment.h

items.body:
## Description

`MBEDTLS_PSA_MAX_EC_PUBKEY_LENGTH` and `MBEDTLS_PSA_MAX_EC_KEY_PAIR_LENGTH` are only defined when `MBEDTLS_PSA_CRYPTO_CLIENT` is enabled (via `psa_util_internal.h`). However, the `PK_EXPORT_KEYS_ON_THE_STACK` block in `pk_internal.h` used them unconditionally, triggering `-Wundef` warnings when compiling with a config that does not enable PSA.

Guard the PSA-specific size macros with `#if defined(MBEDTLS_PSA_CRYPTO_CLIENT)`, falling back to 0 when PSA is disabled. The subsequent `MBEDTLS_ECP_MAX_BYTES` and `MBEDTLS_ECP_MAX_PT_LEN` comparisons then set the proper buffer size.

This issue does not affect the development branch, where `psa_util_internal.h` is included unconditionally.

### Reproduce

Create a minimal config without PSA (e.g., AES + SHA256 only):


```
cat  /tmp/minimal_config.h

#define MBEDTLS_AES_C
#define MBEDTLS_SHA256_C
#define MBEDTLS_CIPHER_C
```

```
gcc -fsyntax-only -Wundef -Werror \
    -DMBEDTLS_CONFIG_FILE='"/tmp/minimal_config.h"' \
    -I include -I library \
    library/pk_ecc.c
```

### Testing

Build and SSL test suites pass with default config.

## PR checklist

- [ ] **changelog** not required because: trivial warning fix, no behavior change
- [ ] **framework PR** not required
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: not affected (psa_util_internal.h included unconditionally)
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: not affected
- [ ] **mbedtls development PR** not required because: not affected
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: not affected
- [x] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** provided (this PR)
- **tests** not required because: compile-time warning fix only, no runtime behavior change

## Description
The PR hardens TLS 1.3 EncryptedExtensions parsing on the client side. The parser now verifies that it consumes the full `buf..end` range after processing the internal extensions vector, and rejects messages with trailing bytes by sending `decode_error` and returning `MBEDTLS_ERR_SSL_DECODE_ERROR`.

## PR checklist

items.title: Missing assignment certificate request context len

items.body:
In PSA-client-only builds (MBEDTLS_PSA_CRYPTO_C disabled, e.g. NS-side mbedtls dispatching to a secure PSA service like TF-M), the local ECP_LIGHT cascade does not run and MBEDTLS_ECP_MAX_BYTES collapses to 1. The `_pms_ecdh` and `_pms_ecdhe_psk` members of `union mbedtls_ssl_premaster_secret` then declare 1-byte arrays. In builds where only ECDHE-RSA / ECDHE-ECDSA is enabled (no PSK, no ECJPAKE), the whole union ends up that small, and psa_raw_key_agreement() returns PSA_ERROR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL during the TLS 1.2 ECDHE handshake. The handshake aborts as MBEDTLS_ERR_SSL_HW_ACCEL_FAILED with no path to success in this configuration.

Introduce MBEDTLS_SSL_PMS_ECDH_SIZE as max(PSA_RAW_KEY_AGREEMENT_OUTPUT_MAX_SIZE, MBEDTLS_ECP_MAX_BYTES) so both legacy ECP and PSA-client builds get a sufficient buffer. PSA_RAW_KEY_AGREEMENT_OUTPUT_MAX_SIZE is always defined, sized by the enabled PSA_WANT_ECC_* curves.

Fixes #10757.

## Description

Fixes TLS 1.2 ECDHE handshakes silently failing on PSA-client-only Mbed TLS builds (NS-side dispatching to a secure PSA provider such as TF-M). In that configuration `MBEDTLS_ECP_MAX_BYTES` collapses to 1, sizing the premaster-secret union below `PSA_RAW_KEY_AGREEMENT_OUTPUT_MAX_SIZE`, so `psa_raw_key_agreement()` returns `PSA_ERROR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL` during the key-exchange step. The fix introduces `MBEDTLS_SSL_PMS_ECDH_SIZE` taking the max of the legacy and PSA sizing macros, so both legacy ECP and PSA-client builds get a sufficient buffer with no behaviour change on configurations that already worked.

Tests deliberately omitted because the failing configuration (`MBEDTLS_PSA_CRYPTO_C` disabled, ECDHE-only key exchange) is not currently exercised in CI, so a meaningful runtime regression test would require new CI matrix coverage. The change is structurally inspectable: it widens a compile-time buffer to the documented PSA constant that already bounds psa_raw_key_agreement()` output. Happy to add a `MBEDTLS_STATIC_ASSERT` in `include/mbedtls/ssl.h` along the lines of `MBEDTLS_STATIC_ASSERT(MBEDTLS_SSL_PMS_ECDH_SIZE >= PSA_RAW_KEY_AGREEMENT_OUTPUT_MAX_SIZE, ...)` if reviewers prefer a compile-time guard.

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided: `ChangeLog.d/fix-pms-ecdh-psa-client-size.txt`
- [x] **framework PR** not required
- [x] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: the fix is contained in `include/mbedtls/ssl.h` and does not touch TF-PSA-Crypto.
- [x] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: same as above.
- [x] **mbedtls development PR** provided
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: backport will be evaluated as a follow-up after this lands on development. The fix introduces a new macro `MBEDTLS_SSL_PMS_ECDH_SIZE` in a public header, so the LTS backport may need to inline the expression instead.
- [ ] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** not required because: same as above.
- [ ] **tests** not required because: configuration not in current CI matrix (see Description); offering a static assertion if preferred.
items.title: ssl: size _pms_ecdh against PSA_RAW_KEY_AGREEMENT_OUTPUT_MAX_SIZE

items.body:
## Description

Backport of #10715 to the `mbedtls-4.1` branch.

The deserialization paths for TLS 1.3 sessions and SSL contexts have been
hardened to reject malformed input as a second line of defence (the primary
protection remains application-side integrity protection of serialized data):

* `ssl_tls13_session_load` now rejects ALPN and hostname fields that are not
  null-terminated within the announced length, and rejects buffers that have
  unconsumed trailing data after the full record.
* `ssl_context_load` now rejects serialized SSL contexts whose DTLS Connection
  ID length exceeds the maximum supported size, instead of overflowing the
  internal buffer.

New tests cover all four error paths.

Related: CVE-2026-34877 (mbedtls-security-advisory-2026-03-serialized-data); see #10682, #10683, #10715.

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided
- [x] **development PR** provided #10715
- [x] **3.6 PR** provided #10762
- [x] **4.1 PR** provided here
- [x] **tests** provided

items.title: Backport to 4.1: harden serialized data deserialization

items.body:
## Description

Backport of #10715 to the `mbedtls-3.6` branch.

The deserialization paths for TLS 1.3 sessions and SSL contexts have been
hardened to reject malformed input as a second line of defence (the primary
protection remains application-side integrity protection of serialized data):

* `ssl_tls13_session_load` now rejects ALPN and hostname fields that are not
  null-terminated within the announced length, and rejects buffers that have
  unconsumed trailing data after the full record.
* `ssl_context_load` now rejects serialized SSL contexts whose DTLS Connection
  ID length exceeds the maximum supported size, instead of overflowing the
  internal buffer.

The TLS 1.3 session-load tests from #10715 are included. The dedicated
CID-length test relies on test helpers introduced after 3.6
(`mbedtls_test_ssl_dtls_join_endpoints`, `mbedtls_test_ssl_perform_connection`)
and is omitted from this backport. The CID bounds check itself is unchanged.

This supersedes #10683.

Related: CVE-2026-34877 (mbedtls-security-advisory-2026-03-serialized-data); see #10682, #10715, #10761.

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided
- [x] **development PR** provided #10715
- [x] **3.6 PR** provided here (supersedes #10683)
- [x] **4.1 PR** provided #10761
- [x] **tests** provided (TLS 1.3 session-load mutation tests; CID test omitted, see description)

items.title: Backport to 3.6: harden serialized data deserialization

items.body:
## Description

Instead of assuming tf-psa-crypto is always a subdirectory of mbedtls, allow its path to be specified.

This PR focuses only on the build system and mostly on CMake. Tests are not included as this would require updating all scripts (bash, python, perl) in this repo and in the `framework`.

## Rationale

This change, especially the CMake part, is useful when Mbed TLS and TF-PSA-Crypto are integrated in external environments like Zephyr where handling git submodules might be tricky:

1. Zephyr applies some patches on each git repo in order to adjust them for its internal purposes. Using git submodules in this case would mean changing the remote and the reference being used for the submodule.
2. Zephyr relies on `west` as repo management tool. In theory `west` supports git submodules, but doing so adds would add a new management tool on top of what's already being used in Zephyr.

## PR checklist

- [ ] **changelog** not required because: the feature is not completed (because of the test limitations) so it's better not to advertise it IMO.
- [ ] **framework PR** not required: ideally changes would be required also there to make all the scripts able to work properly, but that's beyond the scope of this PR.
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: no change there
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: no change there
- [ ] **mbedtls development PR** provided: this one
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** not required because: it's a new feature
- [ ] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** not required because: it's a new feature
- **tests**  provided: would be nice to have, but it requires the CI to setup a test where tf-psa-crypto is not a submodule of mbedtls.

items.body:
## Description

Fixes #10753.

`mbedtls_x509_dn_gets()` rendered each DN value into a fixed 256-byte internal buffer before copying it to the caller's buffer. A value whose RFC 4514 escaped or hex-encoded form exceeds 256 bytes overflowed that buffer, so the function returned `MBEDTLS_ERR_X509_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL` even when the caller's buffer was large enough.

The value is now written straight to the output buffer through the existing `MBEDTLS_X509_SAFE_SNPRINTF` path, so the error is returned only when the output buffer is really too small. Output is unchanged for DNs that already fit. Regression tests cover the escaped and hex paths.

## PR checklist

- [x] **changelog** provided
- [ ] **framework PR** not required
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto development PR** not required because: the fix is in mbedtls x509 code, no crypto change
- [ ] **TF-PSA-Crypto 1.1 PR** not required because: the fix is in mbedtls x509 code, no crypto change
- [x] **mbedtls development PR** this PR
- [ ] **mbedtls 4.1 PR** to follow after development review
- [ ] **mbedtls 3.6 PR** to follow after development review
- [x] **tests** provided
